suites.name: Config (Task 3)
missing_cases: Race: resolveConfig called while worktree directory is being created/deleted by concurrent scaffold — _findGoalFile readdirSync can throw ENOENT if directory is removed between existsSync and readdirSync
Path traversal: name='../../../etc/passwd' — pm2Name becomes 'ralph-../../../etc/passwd', stateDir resolves to arbitrary path
edge_cases: Symlinked worktree — _findGoalFile follows symlinks via readdirSync, may find _GOAL in symlink target, not the worktree itself
projectRoot on NFS/CIFS mount — existsSync may be stale. Config should not rely on filesystem state for pure convention resolution
Name with characters illegal on Windows (CON, PRN, NUL) — not relevant for Linux but naming convention not enforced

suites.name: StateReader (Task 4)
missing_cases: Race: StateReader.read() while ralph is actively writing state.json — readFileSync reads partial/truncated JSON (ralph uses atomic write via tmp+rename? unknown — if not, this is a real race)
State file is a symlink to a file that disappears between existsSync and readFileSync — TOCTOU race
State file with BOM (UTF-8 BOM marker) — JSON.parse may fail on \uFEFF prefix
edge_cases: Read-only filesystem — readFileSync itself won't fail, but if path resolution is wrong, existsSync may return false
State file larger than available memory — readFileSync loads entire file, no streaming
startedAt is not ISO-8601 (e.g., Unix timestamp number instead of string) — elapsedHours uses new Date(number) which produces 1970-based date

suites.name: InventoryReader (Task 4)
missing_cases: Race: reading inventory.json while ralph is writing — same partial-read concern as StateReader
Inventory with circular references in phases (JSON doesn't support this but a corrupted file could have duplicate keys with different values)
edge_cases: Schema drift: inventory with 'last_updated' (snake_case) instead of 'lastUpdated' — field silently ignored, no warning
Phase key ordering: phases are Record<string,...> — iteration order depends on JS engine, completionPercentage may be non-deterministic if order matters for display
Task status is an array instead of string — type cast at runtime will produce unexpected behavior

suites.name: RulesReader (Task 4)
missing_cases: TOML file with circular reference or deeply nested structures — smol-toml parsing may hang or OOM
TOML file encoding mismatch (UTF-16, Latin-1) — readFileSync with 'utf-8' on non-UTF8 produces garbage
edge_cases: TOML with extremely long string values (multi-MB prompt) — parsed successfully but may OOM downstream
TOML with rules that have 0 entries — rules.modulo.entries would be empty array, no test for this
smol-toml version incompatibility — parsing behavior may differ across versions, no pinning test

suites.name: Pm2Client (Task 5)
missing_cases: PM2 daemon not running — pm2.connect() will retry and timeout. Test should verify error message is actionable, not a raw ECONNREFUSED
PM2 socket permission denied — if PM2 daemon socket is owned by different user, connect fails with EACCES
PM2 process in 'launching' state — status field may be 'launching' not 'online'/'stopped', not handled in any consumer
pm2.list() returns null (not an error, just empty) — some PM2 versions return null instead of []
pm2.start() called with script that doesn't exist — PM2 will report error asynchronously, need to verify error propagation
Concurrent connect/disconnect from multiple async operations — connected flag is not thread-safe (single-threaded JS, but async interleaving can cause issues)
edge_cases: PM2 process with monit: null (not just missing fields) — pm.monit?.memory ?? 0 handles null but not tested
Process name with non-'ralph-' prefix that coincidentally appears in list — filtering is correct but not tested with mixed PM2 processes
PM2 restart_time overflow — PM2 tracks restarts as integer, very long-running daemon could have huge values
pm2_env.args is a string instead of array — type guard Array.isArray exists but not tested

suites.name: Formatter (Task 6)
missing_cases: Zero processes — formatListTable([]) should produce header-only or empty output, formatDoctorOutput with all empty arrays
Very long process name (>24 chars) — pad() truncates but doesn't indicate truncation (no '…')
Name with exactly 24 chars — boundary: pad() should return it as-is, not tested
All processes are stopped — formatDoctorOutput would only show stopped category, output format untested
edge_cases: ANSI color codes in formatted output break column alignment — formatStatus wraps in ANSI but pad() counts ANSI bytes as visible chars
Negative numbers (restarts: -1) — pad(String(-1), 10) works but semantically wrong
Unicode characters in name — pad() uses string.length which counts code units, not grapheme clusters. Emoji names would misalign
formatDoctorOutput with 0 healthy processes — still prints HEALTHY header with empty list, looks wrong

suites.name: GoalFileManager (Task 7)
missing_cases: Race: scaffold creates _GOAL file while GoalFileManager.injectHeader is writing — no file locking, corrupted write possible

Two _GOAL files: _GOAL.md and _GOAL_feature.md — findGoalFile uses Array.find which returns first match. Which one? Depends on readdir order (filesystem-dependent, not alphabetical). Not specified, not tested
edge_cases: _GOAL file is a symlink — writeFileSync follows symlink, writes to target. If target is read-only, throws
_GOAL file with CRLF line endings — injectHeader splits on '\n' but content may have '\r\n'. Reassembled content changes line endings
Header injection when '# _GOAL' appears inside a markdown code block — titleEnd would point to wrong location
worktreePath is root '/' — readdirSync('/') would scan entire root filesystem for _GOAL files

suites.name: Doctor (Task 8)
missing_cases: Precedence bug: stopped process with 200 restarts — code checks status==='stopped' FIRST, so it goes to 'stopped' not 'crashLooping'. Is this correct? Not documented, not tested
Process with progressPct=-1 (no inventory) AND noProgress>=10 — should be 'stuck' but -1 >= 100 is false, so falls through correctly. But what if progressPct is undefined?
Process with status 'errored' — falls through all conditions to 'healthy'. An errored process classified as healthy is a bug
edge_cases: Very large input array (1000+ processes) — O(n) is fine but output formatting may be slow
Process with NaN or Infinity values — noProgress=Infinity, restarts=NaN — comparison operators produce false, process becomes 'healthy'
Duplicate process names in input — not prevented, both appear in output
Threshold constants are not configurable — no way to test with different thresholds

suites.name: ScaffoldBuilder (Task 9)
missing_cases: Race: two scaffoldStateDir calls on same directory simultaneously — both check existsSync, both see false, both mkdirSync — second throws EEXIST
Scaffold while ralph-admin start is running — PM2 process starts writing to state dir before scaffold finishes creating files
edge_cases: State dir path contains a symlink component — existsSync follows symlinks, mkdirSync may fail if symlink target doesn't exist
Name containing '/' or null bytes — path traversal vulnerability, stateDir could escape expected directory tree
Existing state dir is a file, not a directory — existsSync returns true, skipped, but subsequent writeFileSync to rules.toml fails because parent is a file
Unicode in name — DEFAULT_RULES_TOML template uses name in comment only, but if name has special TOML chars, replacement is safe (it's in a comment)

additional_test_suites_needed.name: Integration: scaffold + start + doctor end-to-end
additional_test_suites_needed.reason: No test verifies the full workflow: scaffold creates state dir → start launches PM2 → doctor detects it as healthy. This is the primary user flow and has zero test coverage

additional_test_suites_needed.name: Integration: scaffold + concurrent ralph-admin run
additional_test_suites_needed.reason: Race conditions between scaffold, start, and status commands are likely in real use. Two terminals running ralph-admin simultaneously could corrupt state files

additional_test_suites_needed.name: Error handling: PM2 daemon lifecycle
additional_test_suites_needed.reason: All PM2 operations assume daemon is running. Need test suite for: PM2 not installed, PM2 not running, PM2 socket stale, PM2 version incompatibility

additional_test_suites_needed.name: Schema validation: state/inventory/rules schema drift
additional_test_suites_needed.reason: All readers blindly cast parsed JSON/TOML to typed interfaces. No runtime validation. If ralph changes its schema (adds required fields, renames fields, changes types), readers silently return malformed objects that crash downstream. Need ajoi/zod validation tests

additional_test_suites_needed.name: Concurrency: file locking under parallel access
additional_test_suites_needed.reason: StateReader, InventoryReader, GoalFileManager all do readFileSync/writeFileSync with no locking. ScaffoldBuilder checks existsSync before write — classic TOCTOU. Need tests that verify behavior under concurrent access (even if solution is 'graceful error')

summary: The test plan has significant gaps beyond what Reviewer #1 identified. Critical findings: (1) TOCTOU races in ScaffoldBuilder (existsSync then mkdirSync is not atomic) and GoalFileManager (read-check-write without locking). (2) Doctor classifies 'errored' processes as 'healthy' — this is a logic bug with no test. (3) Pm2Client has no tests for PM2-not-running, permission denied, or null responses from PM2 API. (4) Formatter's pad() function miscounts when ANSI escape codes are present — invisible bug that breaks table alignment. (5) No integration test for the primary workflow (scaffold → start → doctor). (6) No schema validation — all readers blindly trust file contents match TypeScript interfaces. (7) GoalFileManager picks first _GOAL file from readdir which is filesystem-order-dependent, not deterministic. Recommended: add 5 additional test suites for integration, error handling, schema drift, concurrency, and PM2 lifecycle.
